title: Vetula Calceocola.
content:
There was an old woman who lived in a shoe,
	 Who had so many children she didn�t know what to do;
	 She gave them some broth without any bread,
	 And whipt them all soundly and sent them to bed.

	    Calceus inclusit vetulam turbamque suorum,
	    Multum qu� luctans natos compescuit arctos;
	    Jus illis profert oblita apponere panem,
	    Verberibusque datis dormitum s�va remittit.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jacob Bigelow, titled "Vetula Calceocola.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
